category: §6.H clause 6 Leak response — tracker credentials in git history
discovered_during: 2026-05-13 evening — operator's 24th §6.L invocation supplied tracker credentials in chat as if first-time; pre-flight credential scan revealed the same credentials are already in tracked files since 2026-04-30 SP-3a design+plan docs landed.
summary: RuTracker / RuTor / Kinozal / NNMClub credentials (operator-provided username + password values, redacted in this record per §6.H clause 4: no credential strings in source code) are present in three tracked Markdown files under commits authored 2026-04-30. The credentials were embedded as documentation examples + as fallback string-literals in test scaffolding. The commits are on both upstream mirrors (GitHub + GitLab).

leaked_files.context: real-device attestation transcript embedded the operator's credentials as inline backticks in the C02 (rutracker login) test row description.

SP-3a implementation plan includes a Kotlin code snippet `a.login("", "

")` + `System.getenv("LAVA_TEST_USERNAME") ?: ""` style fallback defaults + a Compose UI test description with inline credentials.

SP-3a design doc Section: 'Login to RuTracker with real credentials ( /

); assert non-null session cookie.'

constitutional_classification: §6.H clause 4 violation: 'No credential strings in source code. No hardcoded username, password, API key, or token shall exist in any .kt, .java, .go, .gradle, .md, or .xml file.' Compounded by §6.H clause 6 leak response trigger.
constitutional_response_required: (a) Rotate the affected credentials with each tracker (RuTracker, RuTor, Kinozal, NNMClub) — replace the leaked credentials with fresh ones. THIS IS THE PRIMARY MITIGATION. Leaked credentials never expire on their own.

(b) Per §6.H clause 6: 'the commit MUST be purged from all upstreams immediately.' Practical close requires either: (i) git filter-repo / BFG to rewrite history + coordinated force-push to both mirrors (which requires §6.T.3 explicit operator authorization for force-push), OR (ii) acceptance that the credentials are public-known and rotation is the sole mitigation.
(c) §6.H clause 5: pre-push credential scan MUST be updated to catch the specific leaked strings going forward (preventing future re-leak).
what_can_proceed_without_blocking_on_thi: Firebase CI token setup (token is FRESH, never appeared in any tracked file)
~/.zshrc export of Firebase token (NOT tracked by git)
SSH key setup to nezha.local (initial password is FRESH, never tracked)
APK build + emulator install + Challenge sweep that uses environment-supplied credentials via .env (gitignored)
Linux x86_64 matrix gate on nezha.local
what_is_blocked_pending_operator_decisio: Use of the specific leaked credentials in any RuTracker / RuTor / Kinozal / NNMClub login test that produces evidence committed to git. Tests use $LAVA_TEST_USERNAME / $LAVA_TEST_PASSWORD from gitignored .env — the credential VALUES never reach git, only the references. That path is safe; the existing leaked-doc references are the issue.

C02 + C09 + C10 Challenge runs that record real device attestations referencing the credentials. If the operator rotates BEFORE the runs, the new credentials are used and the leaked ones become unusable cleanup-noise. If the operator accepts the leak, the runs proceed with the leaked credentials and the leak remains.
scripts/firebase-distribute.sh DISTRIBUTION — independent of this incident; the credentials are not part of the distribute payload.
operator_action_owed: The operator's directive (24th §6.L invocation) said 'Make sure these credentials do not leak or get Git versioned!' — they did not realize the credentials were already in git history. Surfacing this incident is the no-bluff response. The operator MUST decide between (i) rotate-now (preferred per §6.H clause 6 primary mitigation), (ii) accept-leak with rotation deferred, (iii) full history-rewrite + force-push (requires explicit operator authorization per §6.T.3).
in_session_action: Proceeding with: Firebase token + nezha SSH key + ~/.zshrc + .env supply of credentials (gitignored). Pausing on: any commit of new docs/attestations that re-introduces the credentials as inline text. Test code uses env-var-only paths (no literal in code per §6.R).
